# spoken-token

> Verifying someone's identity over a phone call, at a door, or during a handoff
> requires something you can say out loud — not a 6-digit code on a screen.
> spoken-token derives time-rotating, human-speakable verification tokens from a
> shared secret. Like TOTP, but optimised for the human voice.

spoken-token is a zero-dependency TypeScript library for developers building
identity verification into rideshare apps, phone auth systems, courier handoffs,
or any scenario where two parties need to confirm each other verbally. Both sides
derive the same word (or phrase, PIN, hex code) from a shared secret without
transmitting it. The 2048-word English wordlist is curated for spoken clarity —
no homophones, no phonetic near-collisions, 3-8 characters each.

Works in Node.js and the browser. ESM-only.

## Getting Started

```
npm install spoken-token
```

```typescript
import { deriveToken, getCounter } from 'spoken-token'

// Both parties derive the same word from the shared secret
const counter = getCounter(Date.now() / 1000)  // rotates every 7 days
const word = deriveToken(sharedSecret, 'rideshare:pickup', counter)
// => 'carbon'
```

## Key Concepts

- **Token derivation** — `HMAC-SHA256(secret, context || counter)` truncated and encoded as a word, PIN, or hex string.
- **Counter** — derived from wall-clock time divided by a rotation interval (default: 7 days). Both parties compute the same value independently.
- **Context** — a domain-separation string (e.g. `'rideshare:pickup'`) that ensures tokens for different purposes never collide.
- **Directional pairs** — two distinct tokens from one secret, one per role (e.g. caller/agent), preventing the echo problem where one party parrots the other.
- **Tolerance window** — accept tokens from adjacent counter steps (default: 0, max: 10) to absorb clock skew.

## API Surface

- `deriveToken(secret, context, counter, encoding?, identity?)` — derive an encoded token string (word, PIN, or hex)
- `verifyToken(secret, context, counter, input, identities?, options?)` — verify a spoken token with tolerance window and optional per-identity matching
- `deriveDirectionalPair(secret, namespace, roles, counter, encoding?)` — derive two distinct role-keyed tokens from one secret
- `getCounter(timestampSec, rotationIntervalSec?)` — compute time-based counter (default interval: 7 days)
- `counterFromEventId(eventId)` — deterministic counter from an event identifier via SHA-256

Encoding options: `{ format: 'words', count: 1 }` (default), `{ format: 'pin', digits: 4 }`, `{ format: 'hex', length: 8 }`.

## Optional

- [README](https://github.com/forgesworn/spoken-token/blob/main/README.md): full API reference with examples
- [PROTOCOL.md](https://github.com/forgesworn/spoken-token/blob/main/PROTOCOL.md): formal protocol specification
- [COOKBOOK.md](https://github.com/forgesworn/spoken-token/blob/main/COOKBOOK.md): 13 integration patterns (rideshare, phone auth, courier handoff, group entry, Express middleware, browser display, Nostr identity binding)
- [canary-kit](https://github.com/forgesworn/canary-kit): extends spoken-token with duress signalling, liveness monitoring, and Nostr transport
